commands.author: samzong

target.title: fix: include gateway plugin commands in TUI autocomplete
target.body: ## Summary Fix TUI slash autocomplete so gateway-connected TUI sessions can suggest plugin-owned slash commands exposed by the running Gateway. Before this change, `openclaw tui` built autocomplete from the local/static slash command list only. Plugin commands such as `/dreaming`, `/phone`, or `/pair` could be present in the Gateway command surface but missing from TUI suggestions. ## Changes - Add Gateway command listing support to the TUI backend through the existing `commands.list` RPC. - Fetch text-scope command entries for the current agent/provider when the TUI is connected to the Gateway. - Merge returned Gateway command aliases into `getSlashCommands(...)` so plugin-owned commands appear in slash autocomplete. - Keep existing built-in/static commands available and add focused coverage for the dynamic command merge path. ## Verification - `COREPACK_HOME=/tmp/corepack PNPM_HOME=/tmp/pnpm node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/tui/commands.test.ts src/tui/gateway-chat.test.ts src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts` - `COREPACK_HOME=/tmp/corepack PNPM_HOME=/tmp/pnpm pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --project tsconfig.core.json` - `git diff --check` After updating the fixture to use the existing `/dreaming` plugin command example, the focused TUI command test was re-run: - `COREPACK_HOME=/tmp/corepack PNPM_HOME=/tmp/pnpm node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/tui/commands.test.ts --reporter=verbose` - Result: 1 file passed, 9 tests passed ## Real behavior proof Behavior or issue addressed: plugin commands present in the running Gateway command registry did not appear in `openclaw tui` slash autocomplete because the TUI used only its local/static command list. Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on this branch, loopback Gateway, existing plugin command surface exposed through `commands.list`. Exact steps or command run after this patch: 1. Started the patched `openclaw tui` connected to the local Gateway. 2. Confirmed the running Gateway exposes the existing plugin-owned `/dreaming` text command: ```bash openclaw gateway call commands.list --params '{"scope":"text","includeArgs":false}' --json \ | jq '.commands[] | select(.name == "dreaming" or (.textAliases // [] | index("/dreaming")))' ``` 3. Typed the `/dreaming` command prefix in the patched TUI autocomplete field. Evidence after fix: The Gateway returned the existing `/dreaming` plugin command: ```json { "name": "dreaming", "nativeName": "dreaming", "textAliases": [ "/dreaming" ], "description": "Enable or disable memory dreaming.", "source": "plugin", "scope": "both", "acceptsArgs": true } ``` Patched TUI screenshot showing `/dreaming` in slash autocomplete: Observed result after fix: gateway-connected TUI autocomplete includes dynamic text command aliases from the Gateway command surface, including plugin-owned commands such as `/dreaming`. What was not tested: No
target.author: se7en-agent
